question: Which of the following is identified as a common risk indicator in a badge environment?
options: Deployment of MIFARE DESFire smart cards
Heavy reliance on 125 kHz legacy proximity cards
Using HF-only readers at all entry points
Maintaining an active card revocation process

question: Dual-technology cards that continue to use legacy LF functionality in production are considered a security risk.
What is a recommended long-term improvement for an environment relying on legacy badge technology?
options: Add more LF readers to improve coverage
Disable all card access and use PIN-only entry
Standardize on stronger HF smart-card technologies
Continue current deployment until cards physically fail

question: What is the first section of the risk assessment memo deliverable?
A lack of a documented credential lifecycle and revocation process is considered a risk indicator.
Which of the following best describes why dual-tech reader support can be a risk during migration?
options: Dual-tech readers are more expensive to maintain
Legacy LF support may remain active and exploitable even after migration begins
HF cards cannot be read by dual-tech readers
Dual-tech readers require firmware updates every month

question: When building a credential inventory for a risk assessment, which of the following should be documented?
options: LF/HF/dual-tech type, likely card family, and whether it appears legacy or modern
The date the cardholder was hired
